title: Ruehssia smithii
intro: Ruehssia smithii, also known as Smith's Ruehssia or Smith's Yacon, is a type of root vegetable that belongs to the genus Smallanthus and the family Asteraceae. The health benefits of Ruehssia smithii include its potential to help regulate blood sugar levels, support digestive health, and reduce inflammation. The bioactive constituents of this plant include inulin, a prebiotic fiber, as well as other oligofructose and fructooligosaccharides. Ruehssia smithii is often used to make herbal preparations such as teas, tinctures, and capsules, which are commonly used to support weight management and improve overall gut health.
taxonomy: Ruehssia smithii is classified as a species within the genus Ruehssia, which belongs to the family Annonaceae. This family consists of flowering plants commonly known as the custard apple or soursop family. The Annonaceae family is part of the order Magnoliales, a group of flowering plants that includes various species of trees and shrubs. The order Magnoliales is part of the larger clade of angiosperms, or flowering plants. Ruehssia smithii's classification provides insight into its evolutionary relationships with other plant species.
